source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Two locations, B and A as shown in figure. At location A, there was a factory power outage due to overload. On the same day, at location B, there was a collision between a bicycle and a truck. Calculate the time interval between these two events observed in a spaceship traveling at $u = 0.8c$ from A to B.

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'as shown in figure'
asset_registry_record.image_manifest shows an existing image asset
multi_step: question asks for the time interval between two events at different locations A and B for an observer in a spaceship moving at u = 0.8c
solving likely requires combining figure-provided spatial information with relativistic time/space transformation rather than a single lookup
multiple-choice options suggest a computed numeric result, but exact figure values are not available in the input text, so score is kept moderate-conservative
verifiability: answer_type is short_text with a specific numeric value and unit
choices provide four candidate numeric answers, making final-answer checking straightforward

decision_reasons: Question, answer, and primary image assets are all present and registry_passed is true.
The sample has high image dependency and high verifiability, indicating strong value and checkability.
Although the figure content is not extracted into the input text, the actual image asset exists, so the core semantics are still recoverable.
